source: Sed, Awk & Grep - Awk command: FS/OFS/RS/ORS, NF/NR/FNR, and the match operators

options: The input field separator and the output field separator
questions.why: FS splits input fields; OFS joins output fields.

questions.q: What are RS and ORS (default: newline)?
options: The input and output record separators
questions.why: RS separates input records, ORS separates output records; both default to the newline.

options: The number of fields in the current record
The number of records so far
questions.why: NF is the field count; $NF is the last field's value.

options: The number of records processed so far (cumulative across files)
questions.why: NR increments per record and keeps counting across multiple files.

questions.q: How does FNR differ from NR?
options: FNR resets to 1 at the start of each file; NR keeps accumulating across files
FNR is cumulative; NR is per-file
questions.why: FNR is per-file; NR is global.

questions.q: How do you print the last field, and the second-to-last?
options: $NF for the last, $(NF-1) for the second-to-last
questions.why: $NF is the last field; arithmetic like $(NF-1) reaches earlier fields.

questions.q: What do the ~ and !~ operators do?
options: Match / do-not-match a value against a regular expression (e.g. $2 ~ /tcp/)
questions.why: ~ tests a string/field against a regex; !~ is the negation.
